questions.statement:
<p>Choose the word that means the same as the given word.</p>

<p>Carnival&nbsp;</p>
The expression &#39;holier than thou&#39; means
Give synonym of &#39;poison&#39;.&nbsp;
<strong>&#39;Abrupt&#39;</strong> CANNOT be replaced by:

<p><strong>Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.</strong></p>

<p>Give and take</p>
options: A competition where the outcome is difficult to judge or predict /
It seems likely, reasonable, or typical /

questions.statement:
<p><strong>Direction: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.</strong></p>

<p>Spill the beans</p>
options: Tell something that should be kept secret /

questions.statement:
<p><strong>Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words.</strong></p>

<p>One who loves wisdom and hence pursues it</p>

<p><strong>Select the word which means the same as the group of words given.</strong></p>

<p>The killing of a husband by his wife.</p>
Choose the correct Synonym for Imitation

<p><strong>Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the underlined word to fill in the blank.</strong></p>

<p>The athlete was <u>excellent</u> in the semifinals and even&nbsp;managed to put on an ___________ performance at the championship game.</p>

<p><span style="font-weight: 700;">Find the best one-word substitution for the below description.</span></p>

<div>A person fond of company</div>

<p><strong>Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom in the given sentence.</strong></p>

<p>He is very impulsive and does things <u>without rhyme or reason</u>.</p>

<p><strong>Select the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the idiom/phrase bold in&nbsp;the sentence.</strong></p>

<p>If you are<strong> fair and square</strong> in your work you will definitely prosper.</p>
A person with bad habits or faults which cannot be changed or&nbsp;corrected

<p><strong>Direction: In the following questions, choose the word opposite in meaning to the given word.</strong></p>

<p>Fade</p>

<p><strong>Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.</strong></p>

<p><strong>Criticise</strong></p>

<p><strong>Directions: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.</strong></p>

<p>Breakthrough</p>

<p>Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.</p>

<p>On the ball</p>
options: Sceptic of changes or developments and thoughtful to react them /
Aware of any changes or developments and quick to react to them /
Aware of any changes or developments but avoid to react to them /
Unaware of any changes or developments and lazy to react to them /

questions.statement:
<p><strong>Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.</strong></p>

<p>To face the music</p>
options: Not knowing the real chords in music /
Barring the entire audiences view at a concert /
Being challenged to a music competition /
To bear the negative consequences of wrongful actions /

questions.statement:
<p><span style="font-weight: 700; ">Select the most appropriate antonym of the bold/underlined word in the sentence.</span></p>

<p>Friday was usually a <strong><u>hectic</u> </strong>day, with people being released, so she was unable to leave early.</p>
Find from below the word that means &#39;esteem&#39;.&nbsp;

<p>Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.</p>

<p>Abolish</p>

<p>Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.</p>

<p>In the same boat</p>
options: To go in the same direction as someone else /
To be in the same difficult situation as someone else /
To share profits with someone else /
To gain undue advantage with others /

questions.statement:
<p>Choose the most appropriate meaning of the Idioms/Phrases underlined in the sentence given below:</p>

<p>The information in the book is <u><strong>out of date</strong></u><strong>.</strong></p>

<p>Being <u>generous</u> is a noble virtue.</p>
